input.command: git remote -v && gh pr view 65 --json url,headRefName,baseRefName,title,body 2>&1 | head -30
input.description: Find the correct repo for PR 65

content.content:
origin	git@github.com:gunk-dev/flux.git (fetch)
origin	git@github.com:gunk-dev/flux.git (push)
{"baseRefName":"main","body":"## Summary

PR 1 of 5 in the umbrella app rewrite. See `docs/UMBRELLA-PLAN.md`.

This PR is **scaffolding only** — no feature logic is ported. The old vanilla-JS fitness PWA (`app.js`, `timer.js`, `style.css`, root `index.html`) is replaced with:

- **Vite + Preact + TypeScript + Tailwind** scaffold. `src/main.tsx` reads `window.__entry` to pick the initial tab (defaults to `flux`). `src/App.tsx` is a four-tab shell (Workouts, Meditate, Check-in, Settings) using plain `useState` — no router, no state library, no UI library.
- **`src/llm/index.ts`** — provider-agnostic `generate({prompt, schema?, stream?, capabilities?})` interface plus `useLLM()` hook. Default provider returns `available: false` and `generate()` throws `'LLM not configured'`. Real providers come in PR 5+.
- **Capacitor Android shell.** `npx cap add android` scaffolded `android/`. `AndroidManifest.xml` has three `<activity-alias>` entries — `FluxLauncher`, `VibeLauncher`, `BalanceLauncher` — all pointing at `MainActivity` (`launchMode="singleTask"`), each with `<meta-data android:name="entry" android:value="flux|vibe|balance"/>`. `MainActivity.java` reads the alias meta-data in `onCreate` / `onNewIntent` and injects `window.__entry` into the WebView via `evaluateJavascript`. Placeholder icons (all three aliases reuse the default mipmap launcher icon) — distinct icons come later.
- **`flake.nix`** — default shell trimmed to `nodejs_20` + `nixfmt`; `packages.default` switched to `buildNpmPackage` and produces `dist/`. `devShells.test` keeps Playwright browsers.
- **Workflows** — `test.yml` drops the deleted node-based validate job, adds a `build` job (`nix develop --command npm ci && npm run build`), and runs the new Playwright smoke test.
- **Tests** — old `tests/` deleted. One new `tests/smoke.spec.ts` boots `vite preview`, asserts all four tab buttons render, clicks each, and verifies `aria-selected` flips and the tab heading updates.

## Intentionally NOT in this PR

- Any feature logic (Workouts, Meditate, Check-in) — PRs 2/3/4.
- Distinct per-launcher icons — placeholders for now.
- Real LLM providers (local/BYOK/paid) — PR 5+.
- Per-tab IndexedDB stores (`vibe-db`, `flux-db`, `balance-db`) — `src/db/README.md` is a placeholder.
- A PWA service worker / icons in `manifest.json` — Vite's PWA setup lands later.

## Deploy note (changed contract)

The umbrella plan said the current deploy is fly.io/Caddy; the existing CLAUDE.md instructions said it's GitHub Pages with `pages.yml`/`preview.yml`. **Both are wrong about this repo's actual state.** Deploys are dispatched from `.github/workflows/{preview,staging}.yml` to `gunk-dev/infra` via `repository-dispatch`. The infra repo previously pulled the repo root as the static artifact; with this PR the artifact lives at `dist/` after running `npm ci && npm run build`. **The `gunk-dev/infra` repo needs a corresponding update before the next deploy will work** — out of scope for this PR.

## Verification

- `nix develop --command npm run build` → `dist/` produced (~16 KB JS, ~7 KB CSS, gzipped). ✅
- `nix develop .#test --command npx playwright test` → smoke test passes. ✅
- `nix build .#default` → succeeds; `dist/` + `version.json` in output. ✅
- `nix run nixpkgs#nixfmt -- --check flake.nix` → clean. ✅
- `cd android && ./gradlew assembleDebug` → **not verified in this environment** (no JDK / Android SDK available). Manifest + `MainActivity.java` reviewed for correctness; the gradle build needs to be verified on a host with the Android SDK installed before merge.

lines: * Single activity that hosts the WebView. Three <activity-alias> entries in
* AndroidManifest.xml point here, each carrying an "entry" meta-data value
* (flux | vibe | balance). We read the alias's component info from the
- * launching intent and inject `window.__entry` so the Preact shell can pick
+ * launching intent and expose the result to the Preact shell.
- * We inject via evaluateJavascript after the page finishes loading rather than
- * via an addJavascriptInterface or pre-load script tag — Capacitor's WebView
- * runs the bundle synchronously after page-start, and evaluateJavascript on
- * page-finished is the simplest way to land a global before the app reads it
- * on tab-switch. The Preact shell defaults to 'flux' if window.__entry is
- * unset, so a missed injection still produces a working app.
+ *  - `window.AppEntry.getEntry()` — synchronous JS interface, safe to call at
+ *    document-start. The Preact shell calls this to pick the initial tab,
+ *    avoiding the evaluateJavascript race where the global may be unset when
+ *    the entry (user re-launched a different alias while the app is already
+ *    running). The App component listens for this and switches tabs.

+        // entry comes from a hard-coded manifest meta-data string, but we still

file.content:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<manifest xmlns:android="http://schemas.android.com/apk/res/android">

    <application
        android:allowBackup="true"
        android:icon="@mipmap/ic_launcher"
        android:label="@string/app_name"
        android:roundIcon="@mipmap/ic_launcher_round"
        android:supportsRtl="true"
        android:theme="@style/AppTheme">

        <activity
            android:configChanges="orientation|keyboardHidden|keyboard|screenSize|locale|smallestScreenSize|screenLayout|uiMode"
            android:name=".MainActivity"
            android:label="@string/title_activity_main"
            android:theme="@style/AppTheme.NoActionBarLaunch"
            android:launchMode="singleTask"
            android:exported="true" />

        <!--
            Three launcher aliases all point at MainActivity. The launcher shows
            three icons; under the hood it's one APK / one process / shared storage.
            MainActivity reads the per-alias `entry` meta-data and injects
            window.__entry before/at WebView load. Placeholder icons in PR 1 —
            distinct icons come in later PRs.
        -->
        <activity-alias
            android:name=".FluxLauncher"
            android:targetActivity=".MainActivity"
            android:icon="@mipmap/ic_launcher"
            android:roundIcon="@mipmap/ic_launcher_round"
            android:label="Flux"
            android:exported="true">
            <intent-filter>
                <action android:name="android.intent.action.MAIN" />
                <category android:name="android.intent.category.LAUNCHER" />
            </intent-filter>
            <meta-data android:name="entry" android:value="flux" />
        </activity-alias>

        <activity-alias
            android:name=".VibeLauncher"
            android:targetActivity=".MainActivity"
            android:icon="@mipmap/ic_launcher"
            android:roundIcon="@mipmap/ic_launcher_round"
            android:label="Vibe"
            android:exported="true">
            <intent-filter>
                <action android:name="android.intent.action.MAIN" />
                <category android:name="android.intent.category.LAUNCHER" />
            </intent-filter>
            <meta-data android:name="entry" android:value="vibe" />
        </activity-alias>

        <activity-alias
            android:name=".BalanceLauncher"
            android:targetActivity=".MainActivity"
            android:icon="@mipmap/ic_launcher"
            android:roundIcon="@mipmap/ic_launcher_round"
            android:label="Balance"
            android:exported="true">
            <intent-filter>
                <action android:name="android.intent.action.MAIN" />
                <category android:name="android.intent.category.LAUNCHER" />
            </intent-filter>
            <meta-data android:name="entry" android:value="balance" />
        </activity-alias>

        <provider
            android:name="androidx.core.content.FileProvider"
            android:authorities="${applicationId}.fileprovider"
            android:exported="false"
            android:grantUriPermissions="true">
            <meta-data
                android:name="android.support.FILE_PROVIDER_PATHS"
                android:resource="@xml/file_paths"></meta-data>
        </provider>
    </application>

    <!-- Permissions -->

    <uses-permission android:name="android.permission.INTERNET" />
</manifest>

input.old_string:
MainActivity reads the per-alias `entry` meta-data and injects
            window.__entry before/at WebView load. Placeholder icons in PR 1 —
            distinct icons come in later PRs.
input.new_string:
MainActivity reads the per-alias `entry` meta-data and exposes it
            to the WebView via a synchronous JS bridge (`window.AppEntry`); on
            re-launch a `app-entry-changed` event is dispatched so the running
            app can switch tabs. Placeholder icons in PR 1 — distinct icons
            come in later PRs.

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<manifest xmlns:android="http://schemas.android.com/apk/res/android">

    <application
        android:allowBackup="true"
        android:icon="@mipmap/ic_launcher"
        android:label="@string/app_name"
        android:roundIcon="@mipmap/ic_launcher_round"
        android:supportsRtl="true"
        android:theme="@style/AppTheme">

        <activity
            android:configChanges="orientation|keyboardHidden|keyboard|screenSize|locale|smallestScreenSize|screenLayout|uiMode"
            android:name=".MainActivity"
            android:label="@string/title_activity_main"
            android:theme="@style/AppTheme.NoActionBarLaunch"
            android:launchMode="singleTask"
            android:exported="true" />

        <!--
            Three launcher aliases all point at MainActivity. The launcher shows
            three icons; under the hood it's one APK / one process / shared storage.
            MainActivity reads the per-alias `entry` meta-data and injects
            window.__entry before/at WebView load. Placeholder icons in PR 1 —
            distinct icons come in later PRs.
        -->
        <activity-alias
            android:name=".FluxLauncher"
            android:targetActivity=".MainActivity"
            android:icon="@mipmap/ic_launcher"
            android:roundIcon="@mipmap/ic_launcher_round"
            android:label="Flux"
            android:exported="true">
            <intent-filter>
                <action android:name="android.intent.action.MAIN" />
                <category android:name="android.intent.category.LAUNCHER" />
            </intent-filter>
            <meta-data android:name="entry" android:value="flux" />
        </activity-alias>

        <activity-alias
            android:name=".VibeLauncher"
            android:targetActivity=".MainActivity"
            android:icon="@mipmap/ic_launcher"
            android:roundIcon="@mipmap/ic_launcher_round"
            android:label="Vibe"
            android:exported="true">
            <intent-filter>
                <action android:name="android.intent.action.MAIN" />
                <category android:name="android.intent.category.LAUNCHER" />
            </intent-filter>
            <meta-data android:name="entry" android:value="vibe" />
        </activity-alias>

        <activity-alias
            android:name=".BalanceLauncher"
            android:targetActivity=".MainActivity"
            android:icon="@mipmap/ic_launcher"
            android:roundIcon="@mipmap/ic_launcher_round"
            android:label="Balance"
            android:exported="true">
            <intent-filter>
                <action android:name="android.intent.action.MAIN" />
                <category android:name="android.intent.category.LAUNCHER" />
            </intent-filter>
            <meta-data android:name="entry" android:value="balance" />
        </activity-alias>

        <provider
            android:name="androidx.core.content.FileProvider"
            android:authorities="${applicationId}.fileprovider"
            android:exported="false"
            android:grantUriPermissions="true">
            <meta-data
                android:name="android.support.FILE_PROVIDER_PATHS"
                android:resource="@xml/file_paths"></meta-data>
        </provider>
    </application>

    <!-- Permissions -->

    <uses-permission android:name="android.permission.INTERNET" />
</manifest>

tool_use_result.originalFile:
# Flux

Umbrella app for Workouts, Meditate, and Check-in. Ships as a web PWA at
[flux.gunk.dev](https://flux.gunk.dev) and as a single Android APK with three
launcher icons (one per tab).

See `docs/UMBRELLA-PLAN.md` for the plan and `docs/VISION.md` / `docs/DESIGN.md`
for product intent.

## Stack

- Vite + Preact + TypeScript + Tailwind CSS
- Capacitor for the native Android shell (`@capacitor/{core,cli,android,app,haptics}`)
- Playwright for e2e tests
- No router, no state library, no UI component library

## Development

All commands run inside `nix develop` (nix is mandatory per `CLAUDE.md`):

```bash
nix develop --command npm ci          # install deps
nix develop --command npm run dev     # Vite dev server on :3030
nix develop --command npm run build   # production build to dist/
```

E2E tests use a separate shell that ships Playwright browsers:

```bash
nix develop .#test --command npx playwright test
```

## Android

```bash
nix develop --command npm run build
npx cap sync android
cd android && ./gradlew assembleDebug
```

The Android build requires a host-installed Android SDK + JDK; it is not run
in CI yet.

## Project layout

```
├── index.html              # Vite entry
├── src/
│   ├── main.tsx            # Preact entry; reads window.__entry
│   ├── App.tsx             # Tab shell
│   ├── tabs/               # Workouts, Meditate, Checkin, Settings
│   ├── llm/                # Provider-agnostic generate() interface
│   └── db/                 # Per-tab IndexedDB stores (added in feature PRs)
├── android/                # Capacitor-scaffolded Android project
├── tests/smoke.spec.ts     # Playwright smoke test
└── capacitor.config.ts
```

lines: +│   ├── main.tsx            # Preact entry; reads window.AppEntry bridge

input.command:
git add README.md android/app/src/androidTest/java/com/getcapacitor/myapp/ExampleInstrumentedTest.java android/app/src/main/AndroidManifest.xml android/app/src/main/java/dev/gunk/flux/MainActivity.java src/App.tsx src/llm/index.ts src/main.tsx && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
scaffold: address PR review — JS bridge, reactive useLLM, intent re-entry

- MainActivity: replace racy evaluateJavascript with a synchronous
  `window.AppEntry` JS interface that the bundle reads at startup, and
  dispatch a `app-entry-changed` CustomEvent on onNewIntent so the
  running app can switch tabs when the user re-launches a different
  alias.
- main.tsx: read the entry from `window.AppEntry.getEntry()` first,
  fall back to `window.__entry` (web preview), default to flux.
- App.tsx: listen for `app-entry-changed` and update the active tab.
- llm: make `useLLM` reactive — subscribe to provider changes so
  components re-render when `configure()` swaps the active provider.
- ExampleInstrumentedTest: assert against the real applicationId
  (`dev.gunk.flux`) instead of the Capacitor template default.
EOF
)"

input.command: gh api repos/gunk-dev/flux/pulls/65/comments/3255370698/replies -f body='Switched to a synchronous `window.AppEntry` JS interface (`addJavascriptInterface`) — the Preact shell reads it at startup, no race against `evaluateJavascript`. `onNewIntent` now dispatches an `app-entry-changed` `CustomEvent` and `App.tsx` listens for it to switch tabs while the app is already running.' 2>&1 | tail -5

gh api repos/gunk-dev/flux/pulls/65/comments/3255370699/replies -f body='`resolveEntry()` now reads from `window.AppEntry.getEntry()` first — that bridge is registered before the bundle runs (no race) and the `window.__entry` path is kept only as a web-preview fallback.' 2>&1 | tail -3

gh api repos/gunk-dev/flux/pulls/65/comments/3255370706/replies -f body='`useLLM` now tracks the provider in `useState` and subscribes to a module-level subscriber set; `configure()` notifies subscribers so any component using the hook re-renders when the active provider changes.' 2>&1 | tail -2

content.text:
All five review comments addressed, committed (e9f67c9), pushed, and replied to.

Summary of changes:
- **MainActivity.java**: Replaced racy `evaluateJavascript` injection with a synchronous `window.AppEntry.getEntry()` JS bridge (`addJavascriptInterface`); `onNewIntent` now dispatches an `app-entry-changed` `CustomEvent`.
- **src/main.tsx**: `resolveEntry()` reads the bridge first, falls back to `window.__entry` for web preview.
- **src/App.tsx**: New `useEffect` listens for `app-entry-changed` and switches the active tab.
- **src/llm/index.ts**: `useLLM` is now reactive — components re-render when `configure()` swaps the provider via a subscriber set.
- **ExampleInstrumentedTest.java**: Assertion fixed to `dev.gunk.flux`.
- Plus minor doc/comment refresh in `AndroidManifest.xml` + `README.md` so they describe the new bridge.

Build (`npm run build`) and the Playwright smoke test (`npm test`) both pass.

Summary of changes:
- **MainActivity.java**: Replaced racy `evaluateJavascript` injection with a synchronous `window.AppEntry.getEntry()` JS bridge (`addJavascriptInterface`); `onNewIntent` now dispatches an `app-entry-changed` `CustomEvent`.
- **src/main.tsx**: `resolveEntry()` reads the bridge first, falls back to `window.__entry` for web preview.
- **src/App.tsx**: New `useEffect` listens for `app-entry-changed` and switches the active tab.
- **src/llm/index.ts**: `useLLM` is now reactive — components re-render when `configure()` swaps the provider via a subscriber set.
- **ExampleInstrumentedTest.java**: Assertion fixed to `dev.gunk.flux`.
- Plus minor doc/comment refresh in `AndroidManifest.xml` + `README.md` so they describe the new bridge.

Build (`npm run build`) and the Playwright smoke test (`npm test`) both pass.
